Fun setup. Although I almost flipped the table when on turn 8 Carnage feasted on the 7-cost Sue Storm card which I just recruited in turn 7...
This probably prolonged the game by quite a few turns. Still ended up with a deck of 8 and a deck of 6 cards after all the feast, ko and undercover actions. Had no time to play another game (even despite flying this setup abroad for holiday traveling)

Both games were good, could have been a bit luckier both times. But that's kind of the nature with Feast--games are a bit more random. Still, lots of fun! I was excited when the 3 picked heroes this game were 3 of my favorites.

For the record, the submitted game was an AntDude and PowerPupa game. AntDude's Mockingbird-focused deck got three Spymasters and Infinity Formula leading to a big finish. PowerPupa went strong with Angel and Jean Grey and was more consistent throughout the game. A fun setup after a busy family week!
Shield level is op. Decks got so thin right hand only had a 5 card deck.

We had two very different games going on, but both pretty close in terms of score. First game we got Hydra level to 10 and were one twist away from loosing but feasting was kept under control. On the second game our KO pile was huge, but Hydra level remained below 6. Domino's versatility was very useful on game one, but we barely saw her on game 2.

I think I could have chosen more interesting heroes, last time there was a ton of mockingbird so I'm kinda sick of her even though she's good. Nightcrawler popped off though and jean grey is different, not a conventional pick.

column36: Adam Warlock kept me on my toes during the game. The Cuckoos and M were a solid hero choice, they let me ramp up my atk quickly for low cost.
The scheme was fairly tough. Our first game ended because of the amount of early scheme twist we had. Our second game was much smoother
Clones on the left and shards on the right. Got to stretch my legs with some new heroes and really enjoyed this.

Heroes had great synergies, our last turn killed the mastermind four times and the whole deck went into it. Grey heroes were KOed fast, Adam Warlock had neat synergies with the rest of the deck, the MM was fun, the scheme was fun.
1 star missing, because we felt this setup is almost unlosable. It never felt close.

column36: This was a slow start and a little frustrating at first but one good turn wiped the city and gave me a ton of breathing room. Wasn’t able to efficiently KO many starters but the decks seemed to work well. Winning in only 25 turns seemed ok!

Great setup with some of my favourite sets! I had a clone deck with multipleman and shatterstars rare on the right hand, and a green team deck with strong guy, Captain marvel, and quasars rare on the left hand. Having a combined symbiote villain prey, making it more difficult to defeat before it finishes the prey was a very neat cross set interaction! Game 2 went longer and messier then the first, but still managed to win in the end with shard deck and multiple man support on the right hand, and once again, green team on the left hand.
I would have liked to have gotten up to speed a little faster, but that was pretty fun.  The scheme had me skating on the edge of disaster through both games.
Fun game, just managed to play a second attempt in time! Had some fun combos with strength heroes and big recruit turns with Lady Thor!

I'm surprised that the second game was the better one because I got more Captain Marvel scaling in the first game. But the second game saw earlier Shards, through better handling the Scheme, and Lady Thor Uncommons, which apparently provided a slight edge. CM Draw common into LT Uncommon into CM Rare, set up by Adam Warlock's green common, were the heavy hits that cleared the MMs and allowed for the win.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.

column36: The other times I have played against the The "Turn the Soul of Adam Warlock" I lost to not being able to turn him. I figured I had to try to kill Bastion ASAP and hope I didn't pull to many of the 14 twists and have a lucky once or twice that I could actually mitigate the scheme so the corruption stack didn't fill up and kill me. It wasn't until this time through a game with the Sentinel Squad ONE that I realized how good it was that let you KO heroes and then slip back into the deck. So with the shuffling from them coming back out and cloning you can essentially keep the deck fuller and reduce the risk of pulling scheme twists. (Which was my goal.)  I really enjoyed this set up and I will likely go through it again.

column36: Fun game focused on top deck manipulation. Managed to get a good spidey deck on one side and a solid Clea deck on the other.

Thank you for the setup. First tournament for me. I like that the chosen heroes created a challenge to defeat the mastermind. In the second run I only bought one spider-man card and never went back to him again.

First game went terribly, didn’t even bother to record. Second game was much better, with the left hand able to build a really strong deck around Mockingbird, acquiring her rare “Infinity Formula” and snowballing the rest of the game. The right hand was a pretty decent Spidey deck that had some decent top deck manipulation, and held its own, even if not able to hit the MM.
Scheme/mastermind was fine, not to difficult but also not that interesting. Was able to pull off Scarlet Spider and YoYo for the win.

I was trying to build a deck manipulating set of heroes, but wasn't really happy with the results. However, I was able to use and 'survive' all the demonic bargains I had the chance to make. I also had a pretty bad shuffle for the hero deck, the ONLY rare card I saw in BOTH games was a two-cost Spidey! I was thinking about bringing two op shield heroes for my second game, but decided to hold on to them for (hopefully) later.

Super fun setup! On the 1st attempt we won on the last possible turn. Vishanti and Moon Knight were great picks as they comboed well with Iron Man Noir and Clea. Symbiote Spiderman was terrible in this setup and always mucked up the HQ.
Not much damage on my selected heroes and relied on Clea too much in game 1.  Angel Noir was the mvp on attempt 2.
Tough games. We almost won the second one. Had the MM down to two
Good first round

Quick game but, fun variation to the normal setup.
Soul Gem is really dang strong
The shards and infinity stones really saved the day but they take awhile to get going. Choosing which villain deck to draw from was nerve wracking.
choose Dr. Strange Supreme and Hellcat to focus in on the yellow triggers, didn't wotk quite as well as anticipated, but well enough, bit of a rough start led to a few escpaes. but a fun game overall

This was VERY challenging! First game was close to getting mastermind but then scheme changed to chthon and toasted us. The second go round worked much better in card draw and we pulled a win. Keys to success involved one player going hard on covert cards (mainly widow and guardian). Player two was also able to get guardian and voodoo power cards which helped synch the win near the end. Both players peppered in some liberate synergies and kept a hard focus on saving captured civilians PRONTO before the “hunt for victims” sent them to the sacrificial chthon pile. This was a very well designed scenario and extremely challenging!
Very fun scheme and setup! We liked balancing Attack and Recruit, it was not as easy as it first looked.

column36: That was a great setup. Took me a while to gain some momentum. I thought I was finished when I drew Madelyne's capture 5 bustanders tactic followed by a master strike on the next turn for 9 bystanders stacked but I managed to burn that down quickly with Venompool's Play to the Crowd. Was fun!
Chivalrous Duel almost had us. Made it difficult at most times. Enjoyed the heros though.

It was really close because I was having trouble clearing the goblins from Madelyne and still having enough fight to hit her without it occuring during a chivalrous duel. All in all a great first try at a tournament.

I went with the game where I Built the Suit three times, defeated the Warbound's trap, took out Wasteland Hulk as soon as he appeared, and won the game in as many turns as I have fingers and toes.  I did not go with the game where Wasteland Hulk swoled up huge and pummeled me into the dirt.

column36: Had some really satisfying synergies! First time playing with Black Panther expansion, and White Wolf and Princess Shuri were really fun! Extra satisfying that I acquired my Black Panther set as part of the giveaway at the end of last tournament. Thanks again! I had a super satisfying turn where I fought Wasteland Hulk and it was the tactic which lets recruit a tech hero for free. I recruited Hulkbuster's rare, but it was for the player who had not been recruiting Hulkbuster cards. Never fear! Mandarin's ring which lets you pass a card from your discard pile was in the city, and I had enough fight left to pass the rare to the player who WAS recruiting Hulkbusters! Saweeeet! Fun setup and synergies with the Masterminds chosen etc. Was able to knock out Wasteland Hulk before he acquired all his tactics from the Scheme, making the game much more manageable.

We had a lot of fun with this setup. The first round we thought we were doing okay but the scheme twists hit hard and fast and we died to them, no escapes and decent points though. Second time up we went with the same heroes and had 2 masterstrikes in the first couple of rounds which wasn't the worst thing which could happen. We went hard with Technology and with Apocalyptic Kitty Pride that combo'ed well.

My first game, I scored more points because all of Wasteland Hulks tactics came in, but I took longer. On my second game, I was able to take him out before the second twist and the speed made up for the points with still having a higher ppt. Originally I had one escape on game 2 and I was really worried but my final Tactic from king hulk put it back into the city. Super fun games

Hulk Smash!
First game got hit by fast Scheme twist for a fast loss.
Game 2 had a good balance of heroes for both decks to manage the city and beat down the MM’s. Even with the stress of one more scheme and losing we debated the risk of taking another turn to clear the city as both had the attack to finish the game. We opted not to risk it.

In the attempt I didn't use (1st), had four twists flip in like the first 6 or 7 turns.  Made for a very long slog.  Especially since I didn't want to have to final strike Wasteland Hulk as he would have been a 19.  In the game I recorded (2nd), the opposite happened, I was able to defeat Wasteland Hulk before the second twist flipped.  So definitely some swing with the luck.  Was a very fun setup — the Dark Alliance Scheme with two Hulk MMs.

I haven't played much with Ambush Heroes in awhile.  I kept forgetting to recruit before fighting to take advantage of potential ambush bonuses :(.

At the end of the day, in the second game, Attune Tectonic Transducer, Hulkbuster Iron Man did a lot of good work.  Was able to get three early copies into player one's deck.  That Outwit: Smash 2 for 4 Attack really did great for getting early hits on the MMs.

Fun setup by like most timer schemes, luck played a part. First attempt, we saw most of the twists and strikes in first half of villain deck which meant it was going to be all but impossible to win in time. Second attempt, knocked out second mastermind before he could do any damage.
Double MM with final blow rules was tough. Got good synergy going but has a bad swing of strikes and schemes to start. The fight instead of kill on the KL cards was brutal too but fun overall
Had a ton of fun thank you!

Great setup!  My first attempt was schemed out real fast, 5 in the first ten cards and the decks never got rolling.  In my second attempt, I picked up some rare's and was able to strike the masterminds but lost control of the city early.  I needed two more turns to take down the final strike on the Hulk Bros, just didn't come together fast enough.

Great Mastermind, group of Heroes, and Villains. Everything worked together so well while still providing resistance in the face of a bunch of Tech and Ranged Heroes! No single MVP- every Hero provided some help throughout the game.

column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: I've never played these cards yet from my collection. I decided to go thematic hero mash-up with Fantastic Four/Doom/Heralds of Galctus. Good match

My first run was a trainwreck Beagle draw where after my first 2 turns, my entire shop was 6 cost or higher and I just got overrun. Second run went a lot better, especially as a good brainstorm turn got me Hope Returns, which lead to churning through Hank everytime it was drawn

Really enjoyed the setup. First game was kind of lucky in that the ambush scheme never came out. I was also able to sidestep some of ambush wounds.I ramped up Dr. Doom quickly to make short work of Hank Pym, including going for broke on the final blow. Second game was a complete mess. Couldn't get out of my own way, grabbing pretty much every wound available which led to way too many dead hands and couldn't recruit any of the stolen tech.

Got totally wrecked in the first round, Psi-Lord was not useful and I got locked in with 6-recruit heroes in the HQ early on, but not enough actual villains to escape and free things up for a whlie. Round two was the opposite with getting very lucky with ranged cards and having the rares of Doom 2099, FF United and Wasp all at once. Between sending cards undercover, Human Torch removing wounds, and the ambush scheme giving me a chance to take out more henchman to thin the deck, the P1 hand was able to take down Hank fairly easily.
Too much fun. Thank you for the thoughtful setup!

I was quite apprehensive about playing with Horrors because they are so varied.  In the end this made the game more fun!  Early in the game, on turn 5 I got the horror, Legions Upon Legions which says, “Whenever you play a Henchman Villain from the Villian Deck, play another card from the Villian Deck.” Of course, soon THE BROOD swarmed out and filled up the city. Scarlet Witch’s card HEX BOLT allows to play a copy of a card from the top of a player’s deck, this did a lot of work for me.  Having villains haunt heroes in the HQ enabled me to get high value heroes by removing the haunt with attack when I would not have been able to afford them with recruit.  The Wong card, SEARING SHARDS OF SUNLIGHT worked well, regularly producing 4 attack from a card worth only 4 recruit.  I found ZARATHOS to be fairly weak, he helped me recruit ultimate heroes buy haunting them!  THE BROOD are just scary, did anyone finish off all 10 of these?   Clea and Proton were a perfect fit for this scenario. Overall, a very interesting setup.  Looking forward to the next one.
Ranged(Berserk) deck versus draw deck! Went pretty smoothly, even with the first horror reducing my hand size!!

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Fun setup! First time playing w/ Haunt was fun. Last turn I sacrificed 3 VP to get the final blow on Zarathos, hope that was the right call!

We got rocked by the horrors - our first was that every future scheme twist also triggered a master strike... second was draw a 2nd random mastermind (and then get to do 2 master strikes), and third was add another scheme twist to the deck (hence we had a weird number of turns vs. cards left). However, Zarathos was all talk no walk - and just focusing on strength vs. recruit was able to keep the game in hand. Despite thinking the horrors were going to make this an impossible setup, it was actually a lot of fun, kudos.

First attempt was viciously fun. Horrors and traps and haunts left me scrambling. Second attempt was a strong one for me...haunted rares = great power. Felt like I chose the right heroes. Really enjoyed this setup.

First game was a disaster--my first scheme twist came up on turn 1 and dropped our hand size to 5. Then I got two that played extra cards from the villain deck (when Henchmen and twists dropped.) Even so, I still fought Zarathos three times the first round. Round 2 went better, though, w ith both sides gathering big power! Dr. strange paired nicely with Wong on one side, and Photon and Havok did some crazy joint work together on the other. One of my favorite pairs I think I've ever put together in this tournament--I'll definitely do that again! I had two traps go off on me, but thankfully they aren't escaped villains. The first one did end up playing two extra Villains IMMEDIATELY, though. I was really feeling it then!

Setup was challenging and fun.  Never played anyone with Haunt so that was fun to see.  The horrors definitely could completely change the game and bad luck could completely ruin it.

First attempt had a 8 cost card get haunted by mastermind and then 5th round got the horro making hand size 5. Reduced hand size made it difficult to do damage to get rid of haunt and also hit quickly before a master strike made him haunt again.

Overall fun though, definitely had to focus on damage to get rid of haunts and broods and mastermind.

This game had it all. Maybe a bit too much. I haven't played a Horror game in a long time, and they really took their toll on my two attempts. Those damn draw another VD card Horrors. I decided to try to have one hand do piercing energy and the other hand go regular attack. Game 1 ended with zero hits on the mastermind, but Scarlet Witch's ultimate almost saved the second game by really beefing up my attack hand. The piercing energy hand was good for keeping the city clear with the Brood and Blood Frenzy villains. I got the mastermind down to the final blow, but wasn't quite lucky enough to avoid the last Twist.

The low fun rating is a bit of a bummer. I think this setup is really intelligently designed. And there were so many things to account for with Hero picks. BUT... the combination of the Scheme and the variability of the Horrors leads to a more unpredictable game than I care for.
Never played this MM before, got my butt kicked twice!

We expected some RNG on this one, but we kept getting the Horrors that sped the game up too fast for us to get the win in time. We just ran out of time in this setup - a few more turns we would've had the victory but alas, that's the way the cookie crumbles!

column2: Who Wears White To Someone Else's Wedding?
column36:
On turn 4 I got Psylocke’s rare thanks to Polaris. This made recruiting cards easy. Ended up getting 2 rares for free this way (ended up giving them to the other side). Thanks to all the ko ability only 3 greys were on both sides by the end of the game. Selene had over 20 attack, piercing energy made quick work of that.
This game went better than all of the test runs I did in making this setup.

Managed to get Banshee's rare on turn 8 after it started in the HQ. That made the whole game so simple. My wife took the PE and I focused on the Inhumans. She was racing to get rid of all her greys as fast as possible to get all the PE in the world. Turn 18 she double PE'd the MM and cleaned the city with her attack. Fun set up.

First of all, I was very late this week with my attempts (the deadline is in 2-ish hours), I hope I will remember to have better time management skills for the coming weeks.

My first attempt went really well, at least that's what it felt like to me. Deck 2 managed to use Invisible Woman's Disappearing Act that it got pretty early effectively in combination with the KOs available from the Villain Deck to lose a lot of starters early on, and eventually all of them. That was a key strategy in getting to defeat Emma Frost. The first time she was defeated her +5 recruit also allowed Deck 2 to recruit Colossus & Wolverine's rare, which ended up to make the already strong deck even stronger. Deck 1 was laughably bad in my first attempt though. It could help out a little bit in the City and manage HQ to leave the better cards for Deck 2, but only 1 KO for almost the entirety of the game meant Deck 1 could never really help out in the Mastermind department.

Deckbuilding wise I ended up with mostly Sunspot and Colossus & Wolverine cards that did the heavy lifting. Cards that did help, but not directly for defeating anything, were Polaris's Soaring Flight recruit cards (not the rare). They helped me get 8-card turns consistently near the end, with the help of Disappearing Act's Covert symbol. I believe they also helped in KOing the starters a bit, although by then it wasn't an 8-card hand consistency yet. Medusa's cards only really got in the way, both in HQ and in hands. Mirage's card that countered the Master Strikes was never triggered, and I think they went mostly, if not entirely, to Deck 1. Deck 1 managed to get some Colossus & Wolverine cards too, but never got much going for it (I blame the starters for not getting KO'd).

In attempt 1 Selene was a sort of Mini-Mastermind, that got to 16 attack thanks to all the KO'd starters. In the final or penultimate turn (I don't remember) she eventually got defeated, although I didn't think it was likely she would be defeatable. The Diamond Form Tactic was my fourth pull and I think it might have been possible to get to win at 22 turns if would have been my third instead - probably lesser points total though, as I (Deck 2) was overpowered at the end, but nothing near God-like/infinite in strength.


For attempt 2 things went very differently. I felt I had a great start where I got really lucky to Berserk a 1-attack card with only a 1 in 7 chance that allowed me to defeat 2 Sapien Leagues in turn 3. Deck 1 got the Disappearing act from Scheme Twist 1 in that attempt and it was almost a mirrored experience in casae of Deck usefulness, reversed in the case that deck 2 never really got from the ground and Deck 1 had a chance to shine this time. However, it took a little longer to KO all starters this time and also different was that there were many more Scheme Twists, while also notthing was in the CIty. It even went so weirdly that even Twist 11 would not end the game in a loss... However, the lack of defeatable villains in the City made for very awkward turns in which only recruit was useful, as Emma Frost was still too strong but the CIty was devoid of any activity by villainous persons.

Fun level for both games was pretty decent, I was quite happy with my choice for Sunspot, even though it ended up not mattering (Sunspot's 4-cost can put grey heroes on the bottom of your deck to make Emma Frost weaker and get 2 bonus attack for it too), because I had KO'd all my starters, but with 1 deck only. The other deck greatly suffered from that and could only help out with HQ and CIty assistance, which was enough in this case. Colossus & Wolverine's Berserk was sometimes annoying, but at least it wasn't Hyperspeed level of frsutrating and crucial to get high attack late game turns in my case. The Combo between Sunspot's effect and Berserk with an empty deck could manage the most effective Berserks, similar to what putting a specific card on top of your deck could do (but those effects weren't there). I feel like Invisible Woman being at the Altar really made this setup very doable and enjoyable. Without enough KO and bad luck from Sapien Fight effects, this could be a very hard one to pull off and potentially cause many escapes as well. Wolfsbane felt much less useful in this setup, but if you had some other Hero that benefited from Moonlight (as opposed to my Sunspot's Sunlight preference),she might be much better.

1st attempt took longer but got more VP. I neglected to get a copy of Unseen Rescue from the Wedding stack on the 2nd attempt, but it was a quicker, more ‘Headstrong’, game so I went with that one. Also, I took the Tempting Bargain (which affects turn count).

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: The hero combinations paired nicely, for the ranged characters and Karnak really was on another level along side medusa.

Tried not to overthink my hero choices and went with what I thought would be the best KO/hand management. Got way too greedy with KO’ing recruit in the first game which resulted in a loss despite snagging Polaris and Longshot's Rares. Rearranging the Wedding Stacks for the second game and managing to get 4 copies of Electromagnetic Pulse for one hit on Emma with some Piercing Energy put it over the topin the end though. Really fun setup! Managed to win the second game in one fewer turn than the first, which I lost with only the Final Blow left.
Second game started rough with 6 twists drawn by the 14th turn, but managed to stave off defeat and pierce Emma to oblivion.

Game 1 went about as well as I think it could have went at the start. Was able to hit Emma Frost on turns 12, 16 to get going to a quick start. However the Master Strikes and only having 5 piercing energy slowed it down to a turn 27. A bit slower than I expected but without the Piercing Energy, I was struggling to hit Emma so the lord giveth and the lord taketh away. MVP on the right hand was Medusa as she worked soo well together to constantly get the throne's favor and clean up the city though there were times where I could have hit Emma if I didn't draw a gray hero off one of her cards. Tough luck there.

Overall a decent first pod round and I'll see if I can rebound and keep striving through the weeks.
I'd never played this scheme before it was fun.  Good call having one of the wedding heroes be Invisible woman... but why not have her marry Reed?
We made it a heavy black game. In one turn hit the mastermind three times from all the attack generated from other black cards played thanks to iron man. A lot of draw.

Size changing is my favorite keyword, and this pod played right into that strength. Focused on tech and size changing on the left side, while right side went instinct with Mystique and Hulk 2099. Both sides picked up Ant Army so I could donate cheap cards to the scheme. Oh, and we've elected the Ants for president over Darren Cross.

I picked Ant Army hoping to make them president with their cheap cards but those didn't show up, their rare did along with Hulk's. Wasp ended up as president both games. 2nd game didn't see any rares and ended up slightly slower.
Mystique for President!! Hulks on one side, and Wasp/Doom on the other(with some Hulk to purge the wounds)!

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: This was my first time using Ant Man and the Wasp and 2099 so there was definitely a learning curve but it was a lot of fun! I ended up putting up Ant-Man MCU) as my presidential candidate because who wouldn't vote for Paul Rudd? The scheme overall was quite unique and was a fun one. Between Multiple Man KOing almost all my starter cards and sending cards undercover/to the presidential debate podium I don't think I've ever had such small decks! There were some crazy synergies between Wasp (MCU), Hulk 2099 and Venom (Villains) were you got bonuses for other cards with a + symbol. I really liked how this game didn't feel like a desperate dash like some schemes do, ai got to take my time and focus on keeping the Bank and Streets clear.  Definitely recommended this one, 5 stars!

column36: Would have liked a second round but the hurricane in Florida and my upcoming physics exam made it hard. Probably should have sent more heroes undercover.

Undercover did not work as well for us as we thought. There were very few cards that were able to send cards undercover. We really like playing against multiple man. Darren Cross was fun. We found it easier to hit him on the yellowjacket side than the regular Darren Cross . I am tired of shuffling the villain deck scheme. Twist are annoying with become the president of the United States. That was the purpose. It was overall fun.

I had to go back rewatch how Bagel did this scheme just to make sure I didn't get the scheme wrong like I did last tournament. Really enjoyed playing HULK 2099's set as the wounds were very welcomed to be sent undercover. Darren Cross was found guilty of Election Fraud and has been sentenced for life without parole.
In the game we submitted, we had about 6 Chitauri in a row go through the City very early. The Rooftops were incredibly conquered.

column36: This round turned into a headache and a half, 3 twists almost immediately while bank/streets were full. Scrambled the rest of the game to raise votes. Sure I did horribly bit was a fun long game. No time for 2 or I would definitely try to do better

First turn Lilith picked up Jarnbjorn and shot up to 11 attack.  I started with two 6 recruit hands and a cheap HQ.  MCU grabbed Evasive Acrobatics and got just enough attack in the deck and then used Doombots and Weave a Web of Spies to quickly  thin down to reliably generate attack.  Comic side went Dark Memories and split/multicolor cards.  Had a couple bystanders KO but at that point both hands could reliably get to 8-12 attack each turn and farmed the city for a couple turns.
Luck rotation of HQ

Player 1 went with a class bonanza deck. Between F&WS and King BP every card was doing multiple things. By the end Dark Memories was hitting for 5 consistently and King BP did a good job taking the throne away from Lilith. 
Player two went with Warriors five (Hogun, Fandral, Volstagg, Okoye, and Werewolf). The strength/instinct was triggering very well and once Fandral was able to move Laufey to the rooftops Volstagg the Valiant and Werewolf's Blood Frenzy were able to deliver attack nearly every turn. 
Despite Lilith's best efforts, and I mean best (she drew six straight scheme twists on me), she just wasn't a threat for the deck combinations I pulled off.
King Black Panther's Rare came through in time for the final blow against 13 Strength Lilith

This was surprisingly fun. The first game I really focused Throne's Favor with KBP which gave me plenty of time to win but it took plenty of time. For the second run I brought in WBN to give more Instinct cards to help GO pop more and it really helped. I barely ever had TF but I was still able to win in 4 less turns with 1 less stacked twist.

column36:
Fairly easy setup for the first week. Since the Scheme's lose condition required the Mastermind to have the Throne's Favor, I picked King Black Panther to ensure I had ways to pull it away from her. I picked Lady Sif for some extra Instinct cards to power King Black Panther's Vibranium Claws. She also came with Weapons Master which powers up when you have Artifacts, and there were going to be a lot of artifacts in this game.

On the whole I think those decisions worked out. Player 1 got two Vibranium Claws in fairly short order, along with some other cards to steal the Throne's Favor. Player 2 went Strength/Covert with KBP's Heart-Shaped Herb, and managed to pick up two Volstaggs for some significant hitting power - although the Bridge space never had a single Villain in it, so his Bridge Conquer went to waste.

Was an easy win, if a bit slow (as usual for me). Several turns went by with a completely empty city. Lilith only managed to KO two Bystanders until the very end, so she was 10 strength most of the game. Would have had a slightly better score, but Lilith's final Tactic was the one that made a high-VP Villain re-enter the city, and I didn't dare wait around long enough to re-kill it before finishing off Lilith.
Playing with Wakandans made it fun.
Scenario was ok. Found it a little frustrating I couldn't get ahead of the mastermind escalating in toughness.
